message:
fix: resolve prefix-to-rig mapping in doctor checks (gt-2n28)

Doctor assumed database name == rig name, but rigs like gastown, beads,
laser, and laneassist use their prefix as the DB name in .dolt-data/.
Multiple doctor checks failed to consult rigs.json for the prefix-to-rig
mapping, causing prefix-named databases to be misidentified as orphaned.

Changes from PR #3034 (seanbearden):
- collectReferencedDatabases: add rigs.json prefix fallback
- DoltMetadataCheck: use GetRigPrefix() for expected DB name
- cleanStaleBeadsFiles: skip metadata.json removal when it has dolt_database
- StaleRuntimeFilesCheck: build prefix set so PID files are not falsely flagged

Fixes: gt-85w7

files.patch:
@@ -48,6 +48,7 @@ import (
 	_ "github.com/go-sql-driver/mysql"
 	"github.com/gofrs/flock"
 	"github.com/steveyegge/gastown/internal/beads"
+	"github.com/steveyegge/gastown/internal/config"
 	"github.com/steveyegge/gastown/internal/style"
 	"github.com/steveyegge/gastown/internal/util"
 	"gopkg.in/yaml.v3"
@@ -2460,6 +2461,14 @@ func collectReferencedDatabases(townRoot string) map[string]bool {
 		}
 	}
 
+	// Safety net: also mark all rig prefixes from rigs.json as referenced.
+	// Some rigs use their prefix as the database name (e.g., "lc" for laneassist,
+	// "gt" for gastown). If metadata.json is missing or corrupted, the prefix-named
+	// DB would appear orphaned without this fallback. (gt-85w7)
+	for _, prefix := range config.AllRigPrefixes(townRoot) {
+		referenced[prefix] = true
+	}
+
 	return referenced
 }
